spec_match.rationale: The invariant table checks the feature's exact width/style contracts instead of broad CSS support probes.

interactive.rationale: The test runner and per-surface explorers update live results for border, outline, and column-rule combinations.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb now describes the same invariant suite and CSSOM distinction shown on the page.

edge_cases.rationale: The suite includes border none/hidden, the getComputedStyle() used-value edge, outline and column-rule none states, and malformed width rejection.

references.rationale: The page cites the ChromeStatus feature link for 5133099851317248.

design_system.rationale: The repaired UI uses shared CSS variables and avoids raw hex colors or source inline event handlers.

summary: Open critique items repaired for this concept. DevTools MCP verification exercised the test runner plus border, outline, and column-rule explorer controls at http://localhost:3000/v147/css-update-decoupling-of-width-and-style-properties/width-style-invariant-test/; the table reported 9 contract checks passed, 0 failed, and 2 explicit edge branches, with border 8px/none reading Typed OM 8px and getComputedStyle() 0px. Console and network were clean, and the feature conformance route passed 7 of 7 assertions in Chrome 148.
